phase.goal: Record what is already implemented/accepted so future agents do not re-plan completed foundations.

wave.title: Telegram auth, session, profile/onboarding, city/timezone data
wave.deliverables: Auth endpoints, profile endpoints, profile schemas, onboarding UI, city picker, profile current/birth location fields.
wave.accepted-evidence: Backend/profile tests green; frontend tests green per latest report.

wave.title: Pydantic source-of-truth contracts and generated TypeScript
wave.exit-criteria: Any schema change regenerates TS contracts; generated artifacts are not edited manually.

wave.title: Today and Calendar product paths
wave.deliverables: Day endpoint/UI, calendar endpoint/UI, access state integration, week strip, preview/locked states as enabled by current phase.
wave.exit-criteria: UC-DAY-VIEW, UC-DAY-NAV, UC-CAL-NAV gates in verification matrix pass.

wave.title: Readings hub and natal block rendering
wave.deliverables: Natal schemas, generated contracts, frontend readings/natal routes and block renderer.
wave.exit-criteria: Unknown block types are skipped safely; natal overview/section routes render.

wave.title: Horary backend credit ledger and idempotent question lifecycle
wave.write-scope: apps/api/app/api/horary.py · apps/api/app/services/horary_service.py · apps/api/app/services/horary_credit_service.py · apps/api/app/services/horary_engine.py · apps/api/app/schemas/horary.py · apps/api/app/db/models.py · apps/api/alembic/versions/*horary*

exit-criteria.criterion: Credit ledger uses horary_credits and horary_credit_spends.
Old HoraryQuota/questions_used/questions_limit model is removed from live logic.
same idempotencyKey + same full payload returns existing question without second spend/generation.
same idempotencyKey + changed time/timezone/place returns IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT.
Late generator cannot overwrite failed/refunded/answered question.

wave.title: Horary form place/time/category UX and premium processing state

exit-criteria.criterion: Form shows question time and place.
Default place may come from profile current location; browser/device geolocation not required for MVP.
User can change place manually with CityPicker.
Submit disabled if questionLat/questionLon are missing.
Selected city timezone syncs into submitted clientTimezone.
CTA is “Получить ответ карты”.
Progress state is branded/premium and uses long-running saved-question copy.

wave.title: Current automated test baseline
wave.accepted-evidence: Latest user-provided report: 237 backend tests and 467 frontend tests green.
wave.exit-criteria: Future waves must keep backend/frontend/contract tests green or update this plan with explicit accepted deviation.

phase.goal: Stabilize accepted flows with guardrails, grep gates, visual/manual QA, and production-mode environment sanity.

wave.title: Sync GRACE docs to current code

exit-criteria.criterion: Knowledge graph includes current frontend/backend/horary/sidecar/contract modules.
Technology doc bans old horary quota model and records profile-location MVP decision.
Verification matrix includes horary ledger, submit, answer, UX/location gates.
Development plan records accepted baseline and next waves.

wave.title: Anti-regression grep gates for horary and contract SOT
wave.write-scope: scripts/guardrails/* · package.json scripts · CI workflow if present

exit-criteria.criterion: CI fails on live `HoraryQuota`, `questions_used`, `questions_limit`, `left`, or `nextInDays` usage in apps/api/lib/components/packages/contracts, excluding docs/work historical reviews.
CI fails if packages/contracts/_generated.ts differs after contract generation.
CI fails if frontend imports generated payload types from deprecated thin files instead of packages/contracts barrel for new code.

wave.title: Production guard against demo-mode payloads
wave.write-scope: lib/demo-mode.ts · app startup/env validation · docs/env examples · tests

exit-criteria.criterion: Production build/runtime warns or fails if NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_MODE=true in prod environment.
Docs clearly distinguish demo mode from real API mode.
Regression test proves demo profile/day data is not used when demo mode is false.

wave.title: Manual/mobile QA for horary user flow
wave.write-scope: docs/work QA checklist only unless defects are found

exit-criteria.criterion: Mobile QA confirms category switching, place display/change, disabled-submit warning, timezone display, submit, progress, answer, history, and no-credit state.
Any found defect becomes a separate small packet; no broad rewrite.

wave.title: Horary credit acquisition/payment/product admin follow-up
wave.write-scope: payment/admin credit creation paths when owner approves monetization details
wave.entry-criteria: Owner approves product/payment rules for buying/gifting horary credits.

exit-criteria.criterion: Paid/gift/referral/adjustment credit creation paths are explicit and audited.
No implicit free tier introduced.

phase.goal: Move all astrology computation to backend/sidecar pipeline with parity, caches, semantic layer, and strict LLM narration.

wave.title: Golden fixture parity for SolarSage sidecar
wave.write-scope: apps/solarsage/* · apps/solarsage/tests/* · docs/11_SolarSage_rewrite_TZ.md if behavior clarified

exit-criteria.criterion: /v1/health returns 200 locally.
/v1/natal and /v1/range match approved golden fixtures within tolerance.
High-latitude fallback behavior is explicit and tested.

wave.title: Natal/period/daily snapshot caches and invalidation

exit-criteria.criterion: Cold calculation writes versioned snapshot rows.
Warm call reuses compatible rows.
Birth/current-location edits invalidate the correct cache layers.

wave.title: AstroSignal normalization, activation layer, scoring, and flags

exit-criteria.criterion: Canon YAML validates and is the only source for scoring knowledge.
Convergence evidence requires independent techniques.
Frontend receives only status/flags/payload fields, not internal scores.

wave.title: SemanticLayer and strict JSON Today narration
wave.write-scope: semantic service/cache · prompt builder · llm service · validators · tests

exit-criteria.criterion: LLM receives curated context only.
LLM strict JSON validates or deterministic fallback is used.
Banned internal tokens are not present in user-facing text.

phase.goal: Extend accepted MVP surfaces after core stability.

wave.title: Payments/subscriptions and access ledger enforcement
wave.entry-criteria: Owner approves payment provider and subscription/refund rules.

exit-criteria.criterion: Successful payment writes subscription access ledger row.
Consumption order with referral/gift/subscription is tested.
Payment events are audited and redacted.

wave.title: Evening checkin and yesterdayEcho closure

exit-criteria.criterion: Checkin upsert by user/date works.
yesterdayEcho appears in TodayPayload when applicable.
Private note is not logged or sent to LLM by default.

wave.title: Microcopy dictionary growth and misses report

exit-criteria.criterion: microcopy dictionary validates in CI.
misses are tracked and reportable.

wave.title: Additional readings beyond natal/horary
wave.entry-criteria: Owner approves reading type, contract, monetization/access rule.

exit-criteria.criterion: New reading uses versioned blocks and generated contracts.
Frontend renderer handles unknown blocks gracefully.

phase.goal: Keep all accepted use cases executable and prevent drift.

wave.title: Backend tests
wave.exit-criteria: pytest apps/api/tests -q stays green. Latest reported baseline: 237 tests green.

wave.title: Frontend tests/build
wave.exit-criteria: Frontend test suite and production Next.js build stay green. Latest reported baseline: 467 tests green.

wave.title: Contracts and generated artifacts
wave.exit-criteria: OpenAPI/TS generated contracts are deterministic and committed. Drift fails CI.

wave.title: E2E and visual coverage for critical flows
wave.exit-criteria: Critical mobile flows have E2E or manual QA evidence before product-facing release.
